channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: State Dept. officials hinted at Israeli 'war crimes,' pushed Gaza aid days after Oct. 7 Hamas attack: report
item.description: Emails between senior officials show some high-ranking Biden administration figures believed Israel could be committing war crimes with the U.S. complicit from inaction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District debate
item.description: Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District is one of the most closely watched races in the nation. MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst moderated a debate between Democratic Rep. Angie Craig and Republican candidate Joe Teirab on Friday, Oct. 4.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump and Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp use hurricane recovery to make first appearance together
item.description: After making up privately, former President Donald Trump and Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp are using the aftermath of Hurricane Helene to put their detente on public display with a month to go before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump national security advisers mock Biden's warnings to Israel to stick to ‘proportional’ Iran response
item.description: The Biden administration has now shifted its priority to containment, helping the region avoid all-out war between its two hegemonic superpowers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says he doesn't know whether Israel is holding up peace deal to influence 2024 US election
item.description: President Joe Biden has offered some terse words for Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, saying he doesn't know whether the Israeli leader is holding up a peace deal in order to influence the outcome of the 2024 U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court leaves in place two Biden environmental regulations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court left in place Friday two Biden administration environmental regulations aimed at reducing industry emissions of planet-warming methane and toxic mercury.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwanese people ready to fight as China ramps up aggression, ambassador says
item.description: Taiwan's representative to the U.S., Alexander Yui, is vowing that the island's people are ready to fight if China invades the island.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tim Walz to make fundraising trip to California
item.description: (FOX40.COM) — Minnesota governor and Democratic Vice Presidential Candidate Tim Walz is expected to visit several California cities starting this weekend as part of campaign fundraising efforts. On Sunday afternoon Walz will speak in San Diego and Montecito before heading to Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blackface photo shakes up toss-up House district in NY
item.description: New York Republican Congressman Michael Lawler is embroiled in controversy over a 2006 Halloween costume, when he dressed in blackface as Michael Jackson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most New York City residents want indicted Mayor Eric Adams to resign: poll
item.description: A new Marist poll finds that a majority of New York City residents want Mayor Eric Adams to resign after his indictment on federal corruption charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump set to hold 'emotional' rally in Butler, site of assassination attempt
item.description: Former President Trump is set to return to Butler, Pa., for what is likely to be a highly charged rally, nearly three months after a bullet grazed his ear at the same site in an attempted assassination.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan official warns China, Russia, Iran forming 'alliance' after Blinken says 'no axis' exists
item.description: Taiwan's new representative in the U.S. is warning about the alliance among China, Russia, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The US and South Korea reach new deal on costs for the American troop presence
item.description: The United States and South Korea have tentatively agreed to a new deal covering the costs of maintaining the American military presence there.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some Republicans embrace ‘pro-choice’ label, infuriating Democrats
item.description: A few Republicans running for competitive or Democratic-leaning congressional seats are adopting and reviving a label that was nearly extinct in their party: pro-choice.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Obama will campaign with Harris in key swing states next week
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Barack Obama is planning to hit key swing states to boost Vice President Kamala Harris’ campaign for the White House, starting next Thursday in Pittsburgh. The Harris campaign says Obama will travel around the country over the final 27 days ahead of the election.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will weigh Mexico's $10 billion lawsuit against U.S. gun makers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Friday it will decide whether to block a $10 billion lawsuit Mexico filed against leading U.S. gun manufacturers over allegations their commercial practices have helped caused much bloodshed there.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Yorkers think Eric Adams should resign after corruption indictment: poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Nearly 70% of New York City residents think Mayor Eric Adams should resign following his corruption indictment, according to a Marist College poll released Friday. Another 63% of New Yorkers think Gov. Kathy Hochul should remove Adams from his position if he does not resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in Pennsylvania, other key states
item.description: Former President Obama will stump for Vice President Harris on the campaign trail over the next month, focusing on key battleground states in the lead up to Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assassination attempts and new threats have reshaped how Donald Trump campaigns
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he'd spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colin Allred narrows gap in Texas Senate race, but can he win?
item.description: The Democratic congressman is polling closer to Sen. Ted Cruz in his challenge this year, and independent race rankers are noticing.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Matthew Choi, KPRC 2

item.title: Californians' crime concerns put pressure on criminal justice reform and progressive DAs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ten years ago, Alley Bean joined 3.7 million Californians in voting for a measure that downgraded many nonviolent felony crimes to misdemeanors, such as petty shoplifting and drug use, hoping it would lead to a more equitable criminal justice system and help end mass incarceration
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris campaigns with Liz Cheney at the GOP's birthplace while Trump rallies in Michigan
item.description: RIPON, Wis. (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris rallied with Republican Liz Cheney in the birthplace of the modern Republican Party on Thursday as the pair delivered a double-barreled denunciation of GOP nominee Donald Trump as a dire threat to democracy.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Photo shows U.S. Rep. Mike Lawler wearing blackface at college Halloween party in 2006
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — U.S. Rep. Mike Lawler said he was sorry Thursday after the New York Times obtained photos of him wearing blackface about two decades ago at a college Halloween costume party where he dressed as Michael Jackson.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: President Joe Biden has toured some of the areas hardest-hit by the storm this week as he and Vice President Kamala Harris traveled to different parts of the Southeast. Former President Trump was in Georgia on Monday and criticized the federal response.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Helene renews calls for Congress to pass disaster funding
item.description: The widespread devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene is upping the pressure on lawmakers for a looming end-of-year fight over disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Politically charged rumors and conspiracy theories about Helene flourish on X
item.description: False claims about the hurricane and the government’s response got millions of views on social media. Emergency management researchers say it makes it harder for useful information to surface.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: Early voting begins in downtown Chicago
item.description: Early voting for the November election begins Thursday in downtown Chicago, with DuPage County seeing nearly double the amount of voters who turned out early in 2020, and early voting in all 50 wards opens up next Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Kamala Harris is making an unusual pilgrimage today — to the birthplace of the GOP
item.description: Harris will travel Thursday to Ripon, Wisconsin — home to the Little White Schoolhouse, where an 1854 meeting led to the birth of the Republican Party. She'll be joined by former GOP Rep. Liz Cheney.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Marquette poll has Harris over Trump in Wisconsin, but within margin of error
item.description: Marquette University professor Charles Franklin joined the WGN Evening News on Wednesday to break down the latest numbers from the law school's poll about the upcoming presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ray Cortopassi, WGN 9

item.title: NJ lawmakers explore why summer energy bills were so high
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - If your energy bills were sky-high this summer, you are not alone. It's why New Jersey lawmakers came together for a hearing to learn why and to discuss how to solve it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Cook County State's Attorney candidate Former Judge Eileen O’Neill Burke on crime, guns and youth violence
item.description: In deep blue Cook County, the Democratic candidate for states attorney, retired Judge Eileen O’Neill Burke, is looking past the November election and sounding like she’s already on the job.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Vance, Walz address school shootings
item.description: Walz said the solution is more research into gun violence. Vance said we have to increase security in our schools.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: NPR fact checked the Vance-Walz vice presidential debate. Here's what we found
item.description: JD Vance and Tim Walz debated on Tuesday in the last scheduled debate of the election. The sparring was mostly collegial but a number of points on key issues require additional context or corrections.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance: Donald Trump improved Obamacare
item.description: Sen. JD Vance defended Donald Trump's health care record, claiming Trump had improved the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: NY race for US Senate: Sapraicone on the border, abortion, gun control
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – With a Senate seat up for grabs, Republican Mike Sapraicone joined PIX11 News on Tuesday night to discuss gun control, abortion, crime, and other key issues. The retired NYPD detective will take on incumbent Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand in the Nov. 5 general election.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: How young Republicans and Democrats are whipping up votes in key districts
item.description: As the presidential race ramps up in Georgia, one vital voting demographic is mobilizing and hoping to impact the race: young people.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Erika Ryan, Kira Wakeam, and Courtney Dorning, MPR News

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz CBS debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
